## Issues

### Issue #1: CRITICAL — Implement JWT auth middleware and login endpoint

**Title:** CRITICAL: Implement JWT auth middleware and login endpoint  
**Assignee:** Donatello (Backend/Integration Lead)  
**Labels:** `squad`, `squad:donatello`, `backend`, `security`, `risk`  
**Priority:** CRITICAL (MVP Blocker)  
**Source:** design-review.md § Risks § Missing Authentication & Authorization

**Description:**

No JWT validation middleware in backend. Routes are currently unauthenticated. Any API client can access any member's data and perform admin operations.

**Functional Impact:**
- Any API client can call `/api/v1/loyalty/accounts/{member_id}` and access any member's data (security breach)
- Admin operations (point adjustments, location management) have no role checks
- Frontend has no login UI; cannot be tested end-to-end

**Required Implementation:**
- Implement JWT middleware in FastAPI (validate Authorization header, extract subject/role)
- Add `@requires_role("member")` and `@requires_role("admin")` decorators to routes
- Create login endpoint (`POST /api/v1/auth/login`) and token refresh logic
- Frontend implements auth pages (login, registration) + token storage (secure httpOnly cookie or localStorage with CSRF protection)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] JWT middleware validates all protected endpoints
- [ ] Role-based decorators prevent unauthorized access
- [ ] Login endpoint returns valid JWT token
- [ ] Token refresh works correctly
- [ ] Frontend can store and send tokens in Authorization header

**References:**
- NFR3.1, NFR3.2, NFR3.3 from requirements.md
- Decision: JWT auth with 15-min access token + 7-day refresh token (decisions.md)

### Issue #2: CRITICAL — Implement database models and PostgreSQL integration

**Title:** CRITICAL: Implement database models and PostgreSQL integration  
**Assignee:** Donatello (Backend/Integration Lead)  
**Labels:** `squad`, `squad:donatello`, `backend`, `database`, `risk`  
**Priority:** CRITICAL (MVP Blocker)  
**Source:** design-review.md § Risks § No Data Layer

**Description:**

Backend routes return hardcoded responses; no database integration planned. Cannot persist member accounts, transactions, or redemption locations.

**Functional Impact:**
- Cannot persist member accounts, transactions, or redemption locations
- Ledger logic (point balance calculation, immutability) cannot be implemented
- No state persists between service restarts
- Cannot validate exchange rules or rate-limit requests

**Required Implementation:**
- Define SQLAlchemy ORM models: `Member`, `PointLedger`, `ExchangeRule`, `RedemptionLocation`, `RedemptionCode`
- Set up PostgreSQL connection pooling (async sqlalchemy or asyncpg)
- Implement database schema migrations (Alembic)
- Add database setup to `docker-compose.yml` (local dev)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] All ORM models created and tested
- [ ] PostgreSQL connection works locally
- [ ] Alembic migrations run successfully
- [ ] docker-compose includes PostgreSQL service
- [ ] Schema includes constraints for immutability and non-negative balances

**References:**
- decisions.md: Ledger append-only decision
- requirements.md: F1.1–F1.4 (Point Ingestion), F2–F4 (Ledger requirements)

---

### Issue #3: CRITICAL — Frontend API integration layer

**Title:** CRITICAL: Implement frontend API integration layer  
**Assignee:** Picasso (Frontend Developer)  
**Labels:** `squad`, `squad:picasso`, `frontend`, `risk`  
**Priority:** CRITICAL (MVP Blocker)  
**Source:** design-review.md § Risks § Frontend API Integration Missing

**Description:**

Frontend scaffold has no HTTP client or integration with backend API. No way to test frontend + backend handshake locally.

**Functional Impact:**
- No way to test frontend + backend handshake locally
- Member login/account UI cannot be developed
- Exchange and redemption features cannot be built

**Required Implementation:**
- Add `axios` or `fetch` API client library to frontend package.json
- Create API service module (e.g., `src/services/api.ts`) with methods for:
  - `login(email, password)` → returns JWT token
  - `getMemberAccount(memberId)` → returns balance + history
  - `initiateExchange(fromMemberId, toMemberId, points, rate)` → records exchange
  - `redeemPoints(memberId, locationId, offer, points)` → returns redemption code
- Wire up Vite proxy (`vite.config.ts`) to route `/api/` calls to `http://localhost:4000/api/`
- Implement token management (storage, refresh, expiry)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] HTTP client library installed and configured
- [ ] API service module created with all required methods
- [ ] Vite proxy routes /api/* to backend correctly
- [ ] Token stored securely (httpOnly cookie or localStorage with CSRF)
- [ ] Token refresh works on page reload and token expiry

**References:**
- design-review.md: Recommended tech stack
- decisions.md: Context API for state management

---

### Issue #4: HIGH — Implement data validation and error handling

**Title:** Implement data validation and error handling for all endpoints  
**Assignee:** Donatello (Backend/Integration Lead)  
**Labels:** `squad`, `squad:donatello`, `backend`, `quality`  
**Priority:** HIGH  
**Source:** design-review.md § Risks § No Data Validation & Error Handling

**Description:**

Pydantic models not in use; no input validation on loyalty routes. Error responses are inconsistent.

**Functional Impact:**
- Invalid requests (negative points, malformed member_id) are accepted silently
- Frontend cannot reliably handle error cases
- Debugging production issues is difficult (no error context)

**Required Implementation:**
- Define Pydantic request/response models for all endpoints
- Add global exception handler in FastAPI (return consistent error format: `{"error": "...", "code": "...", "details": {...}}`)
- Validate business logic (e.g., point balance >= redemption cost)
- Return appropriate HTTP status codes (400 for bad request, 403 for forbidden, 409 for conflict)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] All endpoints have Pydantic models
- [ ] Global exception handler returns consistent format
- [ ] Business logic validation prevents invalid state
- [ ] HTTP status codes follow REST conventions
- [ ] Error messages include actionable details

**Timeline:** Implement during auth/data layer work

### Issue #5: HIGH — Set up testing infrastructure (backend)

**Title:** Set up pytest testing infrastructure and test suite  
**Assignee:** Donatello (Backend/Integration Lead)  
**Labels:** `squad`, `squad:donatello`, `backend`, `testing`  
**Priority:** HIGH  
**Source:** design-review.md § Risks § Testing Infrastructure is Placeholder

**Description:**

`npm run test:backend` is a stub. No CI/CD gates; untested code can merge. Ledger consistency bugs won't be caught.

**Required Implementation:**
- Set up pytest + conftest.py (database fixtures, auth mocking)
- Write unit tests: auth middleware, ledger math, exchange logic
- Write integration tests: full flow (login → ingest points → exchange → redeem)
- Aim for >70% coverage on business logic

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] pytest configured with fixtures
- [ ] Unit tests cover auth, ledger, exchange logic
- [ ] Integration tests cover core flows
- [ ] Coverage report shows >70% on business logic
- [ ] Tests pass locally and can run in CI

**Timeline:** Implement in parallel with feature work (not a blocker, but needed before MVP sign-off)

**References:**
- design-review.md: Testing section with coverage targets

### Issue #7: HIGH — Create docker-compose setup for local development

**Title:** Create docker-compose setup with PostgreSQL, Redis, and services  
**Assignee:** Rafaello (Platform Engineer)  
**Labels:** `squad`, `squad:rafaello`, `devops`, `infrastructure`  
**Priority:** HIGH  
**Source:** design-review.md § Risks § Deployment & Infrastructure Undefined

**Description:**

`infra/` folder contains only README placeholders; no docker-compose, no cloud IaC, no CI/CD.

**Functional Impact:**
- Cannot run services locally without manual setup (Database, Redis)
- No clear path to staging/production deployment
- Ops team has no runbooks

**Required Implementation:**
- Create `infra/docker-compose.yml` with services: frontend, backend, postgres, redis
  - Mount volumes for code (hot reload)
  - Configure networks for inter-service communication
  - Environment file injection from `.env`
- Create `infra/local-setup.md` with steps:
  - `docker-compose up` to start all services
  - `npm run setup` to install dependencies
  - `npm run migrate` to initialize database schema

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] docker-compose.yml created with all services
- [ ] Volumes mounted for hot reload
- [ ] Networks configured for inter-service communication
- [ ] `.env` injection works correctly
- [ ] `docker-compose up` brings up all services without errors
- [ ] Services can communicate with each other

**Timeline:** Complete before MVP handoff (needed for squad validation)

### Issue #9: HIGH — Document ledger immutability and concurrency model

**Title:** Document and implement ledger immutability and concurrency safety  
**Assignee:** Donatello (Backend/Integration Lead)  
**Labels:** `squad`, `squad:donatello`, `backend`, `architecture`  
**Priority:** HIGH  
**Source:** design-review.md § Risks § Ledger Immutability & Concurrency Model Unclear

**Description:**

No documented strategy for ledger writes, conflict resolution, or race condition prevention. Two concurrent exchanges might double-spend points.

**Design Decision (Already Made in decisions.md):**
- Ledger table is append-only (INSERT only; no UPDATE/DELETE)
- Corrections are recorded as new entries (e.g., debit entry marked "correction for txn_id: X")
- Point balance is SUM of all non-reversed entries

**Required Implementation:**
- Use database row-level locking or SELECT ... FOR UPDATE when reading current balance before debit
- Wrap exchange/redeem operations in database transactions (SERIALIZABLE isolation level or advisory locks)
- Test with concurrent load (e.g., 10 members exchanging simultaneously)
- Document in `docs/architecture.md` (new section)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Ledger design documented in architecture
- [ ] Row-level locking or advisory locks implemented
- [ ] Transactions use SERIALIZABLE isolation or equivalent
- [ ] Concurrent load test passes (no double-spend)
- [ ] Balance check performed atomically before debit

**Timeline:** Document now; implement during point logic work

---

### Issue #10: MEDIUM — Implement rate limiting and DDoS protection

**Title:** Implement rate limiting on API endpoints  
**Assignee:** Donatello (Backend/Integration Lead)  
**Labels:** `squad`, `squad:donatello`, `backend`, `security`  
**Priority:** MEDIUM  
**Source:** design-review.md § Risks § No Rate Limiting or DDoS Protection

**Description:**

No rate limiting on API endpoints; external apps can flood point ingestion API.

**Functional Impact:**
- Malicious/accidental point ingestion spike could overwhelm database
- Members can spam redemption requests
- Frontend could hammer API with every keystroke

**Required Implementation:**
- Add rate limiting middleware (e.g., `slowapi` Python package or Redis-backed counter)
  - Per-IP or per-API-key rate limit (e.g., 100 requests/min for external app ingestion)
  - Per-member rate limit for redemptions (e.g., 10 redemptions/hour)
- Frontend: Debounce/throttle input (e.g., exchange amount field only updates ledger every 500ms)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Rate limiting middleware configured
- [ ] External app ingestion rate limited to 100 req/min
- [ ] Redemptions rate limited to 10/hour per member
- [ ] Frontend input debounced/throttled
- [ ] Tests verify rate limit enforcement

**Timeline:** Implement during auth/middleware setup

### Issue #11: Missing Decision — Design point ingestion API

**Title:** DECISION REQUIRED: Design and finalize point ingestion API  
**Assignee:** Mark Henry (Product Owner)  
**Labels:** `squad`, `decision`, `backend`  
**Priority:** HIGH  
**Source:** design-review.md § Missing Pieces § Point Ingestion API Design

**Description:**

Point ingestion endpoint exists but is a placeholder. Needs API design sign-off from backend lead and product owner.

**Decision Points:**
- Request schema: `{ "member_id", "amount", "source", "metadata" }` — approved?
- Response schema: `{ "transaction_id", "balance_after", "timestamp" }` — approved?
- Authentication: API key header or OAuth2 bearer token for external apps? → **NEEDS DECISION**
- Idempotency key handling: how to prevent duplicate ingestion if request retried? → **NEEDS DECISION**

**Required Action:**
- Review and approve point ingestion API contract
- Clarify idempotency strategy (e.g., Idempotency-Key header)
- Document in `docs/api-contract.md`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Point ingestion endpoint documented
- [ ] Request/response schemas approved
- [ ] Authentication strategy confirmed
- [ ] Idempotency handling defined
- [ ] Example requests/responses provided

**References:**
- design-review.md: Point Ingestion API Design section

### Issue #12: Missing Decision — Ledger correction workflow and exchange rate management

**Title:** DECISION REQUIRED: Ledger correction workflow and exchange rate configuration  
**Assignee:** Mark Henry (Product Owner)  
**Labels:** `squad`, `decision`, `backend`  
**Priority:** HIGH  
**Source:** requirements.md § Decision Checkpoints (Pending)

**Description:**

Three design decisions pending product owner review:

1. **Ledger Correction Workflow:** Manual adjustment only vs. "reversal" transaction?
   - Recommendation: reversal transactions for immutability

2. **Exchange Rate Management:** Hardcoded vs. database-driven?
   - Recommendation: database for ops flexibility

3. **Redemption Code Format:** Numeric PIN vs. alphanumeric vs. QR?
   - Recommendation: 8-digit alphanumeric for MVP simplicity

**Required Action:**
- Review and approve all three decisions
- Confirm recommendations or propose alternatives
- Document approved strategy in `docs/architecture.md`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Correction workflow approved
- [ ] Exchange rate configuration approach approved
- [ ] Redemption code format approved
- [ ] Decisions documented
- [ ] Backend can proceed with implementation

**References:**
- requirements.md: Decision Checkpoints section
- decisions.md: Design review decisions (pending squad review)

### Issue #13: Missing Piece — Frontend state management setup

**Title:** Implement frontend state management (Context API + useReducer)  
**Assignee:** Picasso (Frontend Developer)  
**Labels:** `squad`, `squad:picasso`, `frontend`  
**Priority:** HIGH  
**Source:** design-review.md § Missing Pieces § Frontend State Management

**Description:**

No state management library added to package.json. Frontend cannot manage global auth state or member account data.

**Decision (Already Made in decisions.md):**
- Use React Context API + useReducer for MVP
- Upgrade to Zustand if complexity grows

**Required Implementation:**
- Create context for authenticated user and member account
- Implement useReducer for state transitions
- Add token refresh logic on page reload
- Document in `frontend/src/README.md`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Context API + useReducer set up
- [ ] Authenticated user state persists across page reloads
- [ ] Member account data cached in state
- [ ] Token refresh logic functional
- [ ] Documentation updated

### Issue #14: Missing Piece — Error handling and structured logging

**Title:** Implement structured logging and error handling strategy  
**Assignee:** Donatello (Backend/Integration Lead)  
**Labels:** `squad`, `squad:donatello`, `backend`  
**Priority:** MEDIUM  
**Source:** design-review.md § Missing Pieces § Error Handling & Logging Strategy

**Description:**

No logging library configured; error responses are ad-hoc. Cannot track production issues or correlate requests.

**Required Implementation:**

**Backend:**
- Structured logging (JSON format for ELK/CloudWatch)
  - Log level: DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR
  - Include: timestamp, level, request_id, user_id, operation, error details
  - Use Python logging with structured format (e.g., `python-json-logger`)

**Frontend:**
- Error boundary + error tracking (optional: Sentry integration)
  - Log client-side errors to monitoring service
  - Display user-friendly error messages (not stack traces)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Structured logging configured on backend
- [ ] JSON logs include request_id and user_id
- [ ] Error messages are actionable and structured
- [ ] Frontend error boundary catches and logs errors
- [ ] User-friendly error messages displayed

**Timeline:** Add logging in backend auth/data layer; wire up frontend error boundary in auth pages
